description: Connects the Koide ratio K = 2/3 to the geometric identity R3/R2 = 2/3, where R_n is the n-ball filling fraction in the n-cube. R2 = pi/4 (circle in square, MATH-11 beta). R3 = pi/6 (sphere in cube). Their ratio is exactly 2/3. The hypothesis: K = R3/R2 because the lepton mass parametrization lives on a 2D surface (the torus cross-section in sqrt(m) space) measured in 3D physical space. The 2D-to-3D projection loses geometric efficiency by the factor R3/R2 = 2/3. The amplitude a^2 = 2 counts the intrinsic dimensionality of the torus surface.
thesis: K_Koide = R3/R2 = 2/3 is the geometric efficiency loss when a 2D parametrization (circle in sqrt(m) space) is embedded in 3D physical mass space. The amplitude a^2 = 2 equals the intrinsic dimension of the torus surface. Bosons at K = 1/3 = 1/N are at the spherical limit (a = 0, k = 0). Leptons at K = 2/3 are at the toroidal critical amplitude.
